title: Lastrea concinna
taxonomy: Lastrea concinna is a species of fern in the family Athyriaceae. It belongs to the genus Lastrea, which is a group of ferns native to the Old World. The species is a part of the subfamily Asplenoideae, characterized by its unique leaf morphology. Further classification of Lastrea concinna includes its kingdom as Plantae and its phylum as Pteridophyta. This classification places it within the broader group of vascular plants.
uses: Lastrea concinna is used in traditional medicine, particularly in Southeast Asia. It is valued for its anti-inflammatory properties, which are believed to aid in the treatment of various ailments. The plant's leaves are often used to make tea, which is thought to help reduce fever and alleviate pain. Additionally, Lastrea concinna is sometimes used to treat digestive issues and skin conditions. More research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal properties and potential benefits.
